event_blocks.summary: In a tense talk show segment, the Male Host introduces Blonde Woman Katherine and Dark-Haired Woman Angelica, seated on a beige couch against a brick wall backdrop with an 'm' logo, revealing that Katherine gave birth to a son, Manuel, and Angelica to a daughter, Alicia, just over two years apart, with the on-screen text stating, 'THESE TEEN GIRLS ARE WRONG... THEIR BABIES ARE NOT MINE!'. The host dramatically suggests their children might share the same father, a young man named Escardo, who vehemently denies paternity, asserting, "Not my kids, none!" and "I got no kids," claiming Katherine's relationship with him was on-and-off and she slept with other men during their breaks. Escardo, appearing on screen with a goatee and striped shirt, dismisses Katherine's baby as "too chunky" and claims she put another man's name on the birth certificate, while describing his encounters with Angelica as a "booty call." He confidently states, "I just think Katherine and Helga can't get enough of me. That's the only reason they want me to be a father. I got a better chance of getting struck by lightning than to be the father of these kids." The audience reacts with gasps, boos, applause, and excited chatter as the segment cuts between the host's gestures, the emotional women, and Escardo's confident denials. Katherine becomes emotional, shouting at Escardo, "No, no, you already know! Just like him! Both of those kids are yours and you know it!" and pointing angrily at a Framed Photo of Escardo with two children, exclaiming, "Yes! Why are you telling me that? They look just like him! Look at that! Look at that! Look! Look at that face! Look at his eyes!" Angelica also confronts Katherine, calling her a "liar" and a "slut," to which Katherine retorts, "I hate you!" and "No, you are a slut!" The Male Host questions Katherine about her past love for Escardo, and she admits she was in love with him three years prior. Angelica explains Escardo's initial reaction to her pregnancy was denial, believing she was "trying to get him back" and calling her "lying" and "just a one-night stand." Escardo, identified as 'ESGARDO, 19', challenges Angelica, "you wanted me to go with you last night. What do you mean you don't want to be with me?" and accuses her, "Cuz you is lying!" The on-screen text updates to 'THE DNA TESTS WILL DETERMINE IF 19 YEAR OLD ESCARDO FATHERED 2 BABIES WITH 2 TEEN GIRLS,' as dramatic red and orange lighting intensifies the confrontation, with close-ups highlighting Katherine's distress and Angelica's serious demeanor, before the host gestures toward the photo, emphasizing the central paternity conflict.

raw_segments: The video opens with a dynamic graphic transition featuring the 'm' logo and 'BACKSTAGE' in bold red and white, setting a dramatic tone. The scene quickly shifts to a dimly lit backstage area where Kayela, a woman with dark curly hair, a purple headband, and a black-and-white patterned top, is emotionally yelling at a young man, Kenneth, in a gray sweater over a black shirt, gesturing emphatically. Kayela's loud and accusatory voice is heard shouting, "Let's go! Cousin! Cousin! Whatever cousin! I ain't getting with Lena's why! Why! Go! Get out of my face! I'm done with you! I'm done with you! Get out of my face! Get out of my face! Get out of my face! I'm done with you!" Kenneth briefly attempts to interject with a defensive "I ain't never..." before being cut off. On-screen text reveals the accusation: 'IF YOU CHEATED WITH MY TEEN COUSIN...IT'S OVER!' and later clarifies, 'KAYELA JUST FOUND OUT THAT KENNETH HAS BEEN CHEATING ON HER.' The tension escalates as the camera cuts to the studio set, where the Male Host, a middle-aged man in a black long-sleeve shirt, sits in front of a red brick backdrop with the 'm' logo. He speaks with expressive hand gestures, his voice serious and authoritative, addressing the audience: "Everyone out there who's a parent, everyone out there who's a teen, please pay attention to this segment. Because this is what happens when teens play adult games." He then introduces a new segment with on-screen text: '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES...WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' The camera then shows two young women seated on a beige couch—Ashley, in a purple top, and another woman in a red leopard-print top—both looking somber and distressed. The Male Host continues to narrate, his demeanor serious, as the camera alternates between him and the women, emphasizing the emotional weight of the situation. He welcomes "14-year-old Unique" and her mother "Iliana" to the show, met with audience applause, and describes Unique as a "straight A student" who "wants to be a pediatrician," further punctuated by applause and cheers. The lighting remains warm and focused, highlighting the intensity of the confrontation and the gravity of the paternity mystery, building suspense from a personal, raw backstage argument to a structured studio discussion.

When I got pregnant at 13, I feel like my dreams were gone." The baby Athena is shown again in a home video clip, being held and played with, labeled 'HOME VIDEO'. Unique's voice expresses her hope: "I know that there's another boy that could be Athena's dad. I'm hoping for Richard to be Athena's father." The segment ends with a close-up of baby Athena’s face, emphasizing the emotional weight of the paternity test, as Unique describes her daily routine: "I wake up, I have to get my baby ready, change her diaper, and put her back to sleep. When I come home from school, I have to..." The segment opens dramatically with a title card, '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES...WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?', against a dark, stylized background, followed by a home video of two young women smiling with a newborn baby. She continues, reflecting on her mother's advice: "My mom always taught me that if I'm grown enough to have sex, then I'm grown enough to take care of the responsibility," and adds, "I try to teach my daughter about sex and I did the best I could." Ileana expresses her personal shock, "I never thought I'd be a grandmother before the age of 30, never," and her unwavering support, "Even if Richard's not the father, I'm still gonna be there," concluding, "I don't think any baby should have to be at that age," as the audience responds with applause. Baby.description: A young child with dark hair, wearing a gray shirt, shown in a close-up with a serious expression, reinforcing the emotional weight of the paternity mystery, identified by the Male Host as 'Dayton', their two-year-old son.

Brandy.description: A young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, wearing a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, seated on a beige couch, displaying intense emotional distress, crying and speaking with visible tears and a trembling expression.

Young Man (Marshaun).description: A young Black man with short dark hair, wearing a blue button-down shirt over a white collared shirt, initially seated beside Brandy with a serious, pensive expression, then standing up abruptly and walking away in shock and distress as the confession is revealed.

Audience Members.description: Diverse group of people in the studio audience, displaying varied reactions—some covering their mouths in shock, others gasping, with expressions of disbelief and concern, reacting to the dramatic confession.

Dee (Marshaun's Brother).description: A young Black man with short dark hair, wearing a pink short-sleeve shirt, walking onto the set with a serious expression, identified by on-screen text as 'DEE (MARSHAUN'S BROTHER)' and 'HERE TO APOLOGIZE TO HIS BROTHER FOR SLEEPING WITH HIS GIRLFRIEND'.

raw_segments: The segment opens with the Male Host, wearing a navy blue zip-up, seated on a talk show set with a brick wall backdrop and warm red and blue lighting, concluding a previous segment by stating, "I want both of you to see if you can repair your relationship, all right?" to which the audience responds with a collective "Yes." He then adds, "You'll be back right after this," before a transition sound leads into applause. The scene quickly shifts to a new case, with the on-screen text changing from 'DID MY MAN CHEAT WITH MY TEEN SISTER? TEST THEM!' to '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?'. The Male Host, now in a black long-sleeve shirt, stands and gestures toward a large screen displaying a young Black man, Marshaun, in a blue button-down shirt, identified as being in a 'SECLUDED BACKSTAGE' area. The Male Host introduces, "Everyone, this is Brandy. Welcome, Brandy," as applause erupts. He continues, narrating over the sustained applause, "Now, you know? I've got a lot of kids in college watch the show. And we know that young love blossoms on college campuses." The camera cuts to a young woman, Brandy, with shoulder-length blonde hair, wearing a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, who appears anxious and emotional, then offers a slight smile. The Male Host explains, "And for Brandy, while education was her priority, her classmate Marshaun became her one true love," eliciting an 'Aww' from the audience. He continues, "Three years later, these two are going strong and raising their two-year-old son Dayton together," which is met with an audience 'Whoa!' and applause, followed by the Host asking, "How's that?" as a close-up of the baby, Dayton, with dark hair and a serious expression, is shown. The Male Host then dramatically reveals, "But, deep within Brandy is a secret," as the audience gasps and murmurs. He adds, "And it's a secret that she feels that Marshaun has to hear today. No matter how much it's going to break his heart. Watch." The scene alternates between Brandy’s tense reactions and the Male Host’s dramatic delivery, culminating in a black screen with the name 'BRANDY' in bold white letters, as Brandy's voice is heard in a voiceover stating, "Marshaun and I met in college."

The video opens with a dramatic close-up of Young Man (Marshaun) under red lighting, his name 'MARSHAUN' displayed, before cutting to Brandy, a young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, wearing a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, her expression tense and emotional. On-screen text '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' builds suspense. Brandy's emotional and tearful voice is heard, stating, "Marshaun is the only boyfriend that I've ever had," and expressing deep affection for him and his family, adding, "I love Marshaun very, very much and I would never intentionally do anything to hurt him." She continues, tearfully, "I'm scared that he's gonna leave me, of course. And then he's not gonna be around Dayton anymore 'cause Dayton loves his dad," revealing a child and Marshaun's paternal role. A brief flashback shows Brandy and Marshaun smiling in a lake, holding inflatable rings, hinting at their past. The scene returns to Brandy in a black-and-white close-up, visibly distressed, with the same on-screen text. The Male Host, seated in a studio with a brick wall backdrop, speaks with a clear, authoritative voice, reminding the audience that Marshaun is "secluded backstage, cannot hear or see what's going on here." The focus returns to Marshaun, in a blue button-down shirt, in the 'SECLUDED BACKSTAGE' area, with text stating he is 'ABOUT TO HEAR A DEVASTATING SECRET FROM THE WOMAN HE LOVES.' Brandy is shown again, her face contorted with emotion, as the text updates to 'BRANDY SAYS HER SECRET MAY COST HER THE LOVE OF HER LIFE.' The Male Host asks, "Your first serious boyfriend?" to which Brandy emotionally responds, "Ever." She then tearfully confesses, "He was made for me and I was made for him," eliciting audience gasps. The host directly asks, "What's the secret?" and Brandy begins to reveal, "When we first started dating, we used to party a lot and one night I..." before trailing off, leaving the secret hanging as the video alternates between her tearful face and Marshaun’s stoic expression, all under warm, dramatic red and purple studio lighting.

The segment opens in a tense studio, with the Male Host and Brandy seated on a beige couch against a brick wall, a large screen behind them displaying Marshaun in a blue button-down shirt. On-screen text reads '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' Brandy, in a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, appears anxious and emotional, her expression shifting from distress to a slight smile as she softly confesses, "up with someone else when we first started dating. And it was his... his brother." This revelation is met with loud gasps, screams, and reactions of shock and disbelief from the audience, some covering their mouths. The Male Host, in a black long-sleeve shirt, gestures emphatically, his demeanor serious and authoritative, as he clarifies, "That's his brother D. And D has been keeping this secret too?" Brandy softly confirms, "Yes." The on-screen text updates to 'BRANDY IS ABOUT TO CONFESS TO MARSHAUN THAT SHE HAD SEX WITH HIS BROTHER,' intensifying the emotional stakes. The Male Host dramatically states, "And here's the problem. It could be his child," prompting another wave of loud audience reactions. The scene shifts to Marshaun, shown in a 'SECLUDED BACKSTAGE' area, maintaining a composed and serious expression. The Male Host continues, pointing toward the screen, explaining, "This is a secret. So he doesn't know. So we we're not swabbing him, we can't give him a DNA test. But we did give D a DNA test. So we will know if it's D's baby. And I'm just trusting you that if it's not D, it has to be Marshaun." Brandy softly agrees, "Right, absolutely," as the Male Host adds, "Because you weren't with no, no one else." The on-screen text reveals, 'THE DNA TEST WILL REVEAL IF MARSHAUN'S BROTHER IS THE FATHER OF BRANDY'S BABY.' The camera alternates between close-ups of Brandy’s emotional face, the Male Host’s intense delivery, and the audience’s animated reactions, all underscored by warm red and purple lighting, building suspense as the Male Host leans forward, speaking with urgency, preparing to bring Marshaun out.

The segment opens with a close-up of Brandy, a young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, wearing a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, displaying a tense and emotional expression, as the host asks if 'D' is known to be present, eliciting a 'No' and audience gasps. On-screen text reads '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?'. The scene cuts to Marshaun, a young Black man with short dark hair, wearing a blue button-down shirt over a white collared shirt, walking down a set of stairs into the studio with a serious demeanor as the host announces, 'Here's Marshaun everybody!', met with loud cheers and applause from the audience. On-screen text identifies him as 'MARSHAUN' and states he is 'ABOUT TO FIND OUT HIS BROTHER HAD SEX WITH HIS GIRLFRIEND'. He approaches the host, a middle-aged man with short dark hair, wearing a black long-sleeve shirt, seated in a studio with a brick wall backdrop, who maintains a serious and authoritative demeanor, gesturing as he speaks. Marshaun sits beside Brandy on a beige couch, and the on-screen text updates to 'MARSHAUN HAS NO IDEA THAT HIS BROTHER DEE MIGHT BE HIS SON'S REAL FATHER'. Brandy smiles slightly, then looks down, while Marshaun appears increasingly anxious. The host tells Brandy that Marshaun claimed 'love at first sight', to which Marshaun confirms, 'It was. First sight soon as I seen her. Tried to talk to her at college and she didn't want to talk to me,' adding, 'But I got her, I got her, I got her,' prompting audience laughter. The host then mentions Marshaun's child, and Marshaun proudly states, 'Yes, he's two years old. Wonderful. I never thought I'd like being a father, but I like it, I like it a lot,' as the audience claps and cheers. The host continues to speak, dramatically revealing, 'But she has a secret to tell you. And uh, she feels that she has to tell you now because she has to get it off her chest,' which elicits gasps and 'oohs' from the audience. The audience, a diverse group in casual clothing, reacts with visible shock and concern, some covering their mouths or clapping. The camera alternates between close-ups of Brandy, Marshaun, and the host, capturing their emotional reactions. The studio set features warm red and blue lighting, with multiple screens displaying the 'Maury' logo. The atmosphere is tense and dramatic, building toward a reveal. The segment ends with Marshaun looking down, visibly distressed, as the host continues to speak, and the audience remains engaged, their reactions ranging from shock to curiosity.

The segment opens with a close-up of Brandy, a young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, wearing a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, visibly emotional and tearful. A male host's voice prompts her, stating, "she has to tell you, so tell him." Brandy, her voice trembling and choked with tears, begins to speak, saying, "I love you... we were partying a lot and drinking a lot," and referencing an event "after your graduation." The on-screen text reads '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' The scene cuts to a split-screen showing Brandy on the left and Marshaun, a young Black man with short dark hair, wearing a blue button-down shirt, on the right. The text identifies them as 'BRANDY & MARSHAUN' and reveals that 'MARSHAUN IS ABOUT TO FIND OUT HIS BROTHER MAY BE HIS SON'S REAL FATHER.' Brandy continues her tearful confession, her voice breaking as she admits, "You know I got pregnant with Dayton immediately and... it was somebody else." The host prompts, "Say that one more again?" and Brandy, still crying, repeats, "It was somebody else." A collective gasp and groan erupts from the audience as Marshaun listens with a serious, pensive expression. The camera pulls back to a wider shot of them on a beige couch in a studio with warm red and orange lighting. Marshaun suddenly stands up, appearing shocked and distressed, as Brandy, barely audible through her sobs, reveals the name "Dee." The host again asks, "Say that one more again?" and Brandy repeats, "Dee." Marshaun, utterly shocked, exclaims, "My brother? Are you serious?" The on-screen text updates to 'BRANDY JUST CONFESSED SHE CHEATED ON MARSHAUN WITH HIS BROTHER DEE.' As Marshaun walks away in disbelief, the camera cuts to the audience, where several members react with shock, covering their mouths and gasping. The host then adds, "It happened one time, but she's not finished telling you something," as Brandy cries openly, her face contorted with emotion, further confessing, "We don't know if Dayton's yours or..."

The segment opens with a wide overhead shot of a talk show set, featuring a brick wall backdrop, warm red and orange lighting, and a beige couch. On-screen text reads '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' as the Male Host, seated in a black long-sleeve shirt, begins to address the situation, calling out 'Come here, Marshaun, come here.' The camera cuts to a close-up of the Young Man, Marshaun, wearing a blue button-down shirt, appearing tense and pensive. Brandy, a young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair in a purple lace top, is visibly distressed and crying, repeatedly uttering a tearful 'I'm sorry, baby' and 'I'm sorry' to Marshaun. The Male Host stands and gestures emphatically, stating, 'Marshaun, we can prove it right now. We can get the truth out right now.' The on-screen text updates to 'MARSHAUN JUST FOUND OUT HIS BROTHER MAY BE HIS SON'S REAL FATHER.' The Male Host then reveals, 'We gave a DNA test to Dee and to Dayton and to Brandy,' asking Marshaun, 'You wanna find out? Not now, but right now. Right now!' The audience reacts with loud cheers, applause, and excited shouts. The camera follows Marshaun as he stands up abruptly and walks away in shock, while Brandy remains seated, covering her face in distress, continuing to cry. The Male Host announces, 'Your brother is here. I'd like to bring him out. Here's Dee. Dee, come on out!' The audience gasps and murmurs as Dee, identified by on-screen text as 'DEE (MARSHAUN'S BROTHER)', wearing a pink short-sleeve shirt, walks onto the set with a serious expression. The on-screen text further states 'DEE IS HERE TO APOLOGIZE TO HIS BROTHER FOR SLEEPING WITH HIS GIRLFRIEND.' The Male Host continues to speak with authority, while Brandy, still emotional, looks up at him. Close-ups capture Marshaun’s distressed face and the visibly moved audience. The segment ends with the Male Host seated again, speaking directly to the camera, as the on-screen text reiterates the central question: '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' The atmosphere is charged with tension and emotional weight, underscored by the dramatic lighting and the host’s authoritative presence.

event_blocks.summary: On a talk show set under dramatic red and orange lighting, a physical altercation broke out between Dee, wearing a pink checkered shirt, and a man in a blue shirt, with security and audience members present; on-screen text read '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' as security separated the men amidst shouts of "Push your brother, chill, chill." The Male Host, in a black long-sleeve shirt, addressed the situation, asking Dee if he wanted to apologize, to which Dee, shown in close-up and distressed, responded "Yeah," then explained, "man, you know, you all like, man, we don't have nothing else. It just happened one time," attributing the incident to "back in college" and admitting, "It ain't no good reason." On-screen text indicated Dee was praying a DNA test would prove he wasn't the father of Brandy’s baby, while Brandy, a young woman with blonde hair, a purple lace top, and hoop earrings, appeared tearful and distressed. The Male Host then spoke to Marshaun, acknowledging his upset and assuring him he'd have time to think, before the segment concluded with the reveal 'THE RESULTS ARE IN!', eliciting gasps, shock, and applause from the audience, many covering their mouths in disbelief, all within the high-stakes, emotionally charged environment of the studio set featuring a brick wall backdrop and the 'Maury' logo on a large screen.

Dee (Marshaun's Brother).description: A young Black man with short dark hair, wearing a pink short-sleeve checkered shirt, initially involved in a physical altercation, later shown in close-up with a distressed expression, covering his face with his hands. He is identified by on-screen text as praying the DNA test proves he is not the father of Brandy’s baby, and is heard speaking defensively, admitting "Yeah" when asked if he wants to apologize, and attempting to explain the situation by saying "It just happened one time" and "It was back in college."

raw_segments: The segment opens with a chaotic scene on a talk show set, bathed in dramatic red and orange lighting, as a physical altercation erupts between Dee (Marshaun's Brother), in a pink checkered shirt, and another man in a blue shirt, amidst security personnel and audience members. On-screen text '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' frames the conflict as security intervenes to separate the men, with voices heard urging, "Push your brother, chill, chill." The Male Host, a middle-aged man in a black long-sleeve shirt, speaks authoritatively, asking, "Marshaun, Dee wants to apologize. Didn't that right, Dee?" Dee, now in close-up, visibly distressed and covering his face, responds with a defensive "Yeah," then attempts to explain, "man, you know, you all like, man, we don't have nothing else. It just happened one time." When pressed for a reason, he states, "It was... give me one good reason why. It ain't no good reason... It was back in college." On-screen text reveals he is praying the DNA test proves he is not the father of Brandy’s baby. Brandy, a young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, wearing a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, appears emotional and tearful, her voice trembling with distress. The Male Host then addresses Marshaun, stating, "Marshaun, you're gonna have a lot of time to think about this. I'm sure you're gonna have a much clearer head than you have right now and everybody can understand how upset you are, all right? That's fine." The segment culminates with the dramatic reveal: 'THE RESULTS ARE IN!', as the audience erupts in gasps, shocked reactions, and some applause, with many covering their mouths in disbelief. The studio set, featuring a brick wall backdrop, warm lighting, and a large screen displaying the 'Maury' logo, maintains a high-stakes, emotionally charged environment throughout.

event_blocks.summary: On a talk show set with a brick wall backdrop and warm studio lighting, the Male Host dramatically revealed 'DDC DNA RESULTS' from a yellow envelope, announcing to gasps and cheers from the audience that Dee is not the father of Brandy's baby, causing Brandy to cry intensely and Marshaun to walk away in distress. The Male Host urged them to seek counseling for potential family reconciliation. The scene then shifted to a new case involving Tanya and Bonifasio, with red and orange studio lighting and a 'Maury' logo, where Tanya admitted to a mistake. Later, Tonya, Ronnie's girlfriend, distressed and crying, explained her seven-year devotion to Ronnie and her heartbreak over his doubt regarding their two children, Persais and Emerald, admitting she cheated once years before their first daughter's birth. Ronnie, stoic and serious, confirmed Tonya's infidelity as the reason for his inability to forgive her, despite the affair occurring years before the pregnancies. The on-screen text highlighted Ronnie's claim that DNA tests would prove the children were not his. The atmosphere intensified with dramatic music and audience cheers as Ronnie confirmed they were not living together and he was 'grinding her all the time about these kids' because she cheated. The segment culminated with the Male Host dramatically holding the 'DDC DNA RESULTS' envelope, announcing Ronnie is the father of two-year-old Persais, leading to Tonya's screams of 'I told you!' and their emotional embrace, followed by the announcement that Ronnie is also the father of four-year-old Emerald, with the final text confirming he fathered both children. In a subsequent dramatic confrontation, the Male Host, beside a framed baby photo and Bonifasio's image, declared Bonifasio 'not the father,' eliciting a chaotic eruption from the audience. The segment concluded with end-screen graphics featuring the Male Host and various audience members, including the Blonde Woman (Katherine) and the Young Man (Escardo), with calls to action for subscriptions.

Dee (Marshaun's Brother).description: A young Black man with short dark hair, wearing a pink short-sleeve checkered shirt, initially shown in close-up with a distressed expression, covering his face with his hands, reacting with shock to the Male Host's declaration that he is 'not the father' of two-year-old Dayton.

On-Screen Text.description: Dynamic text overlays including '2 TEEN COUSINS...2 BABIES... IS BONIFASIO THE FATHER?' appearing at the bottom of the screen during the initial scene, and later 'WATCH MORE' and 'CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE' on the promotional end-screens.

Young Woman in Blue Top.description: A young woman with dark hair, wearing a blue top, shown in a wider shot beside the Male Host, covering her face in distress as she walks, indicating emotional turmoil in the new confrontation segment.

Male Host.description: A middle-aged man with short dark hair, initially wearing a red sweater, smiling slightly beside a framed baby photo and a screen showing Bonifasio, whose voice is heard calmly saying 'Hey, thank you very much' before dramatically declaring 'You are not the father!'. He later appears in a black blazer over a teal shirt holding a yellow 'DDC DNA Diagnostics Center' envelope, and finally in a black blazer over a gray sweater, smiling broadly in a promotional end-screen graphic.

Studio Set.description: A talk show set with a brick wall backdrop, warm red and orange lighting, a beige couch for guests, and a large screen displaying the 'Maury' logo, creating a dramatic and emotionally charged atmosphere.

Brandy.description: A young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, wearing a purple lace top and large hoop earrings, seated on a beige couch, displaying intense emotional distress, crying with visible tears and a trembling expression as the DNA results are revealed, amidst audience gasps and cheers.

Yellow Envelope.description: A bright yellow envelope labeled 'DDC DNA Diagnostics Center', held by the Male Host in the 'WATCH MORE' end-screen, serving as a key prop from the show’s DNA testing theme.

Young Woman in Blue Top (New Confrontati.description: A young woman with long dark hair, wearing a blue top, appearing distressed and covering her face as she walks beside the Male Host, indicating emotional turmoil in the new confrontation about teen cousins and paternity.

Marshaun.description: A young Black man with short dark hair, wearing a blue button-down shirt over a white collared shirt, initially seated beside Brandy with a serious, pensive expression, then standing up abruptly and walking away in shock and distress as the DNA results are revealed, and later addressed by the Male Host regarding 'healing' and 'forgiveness' and the need for counseling.

Bonifasio.description: A man with short dark hair and a mustache, wearing a red and black striped shirt, seated on a beige couch with a serious, stoic expression, shown in close-up and on a large screen behind the host, but remains silent during this segment.

Persais.description: A young child with short brown hair, shown in close-up with a pouting expression, later appearing in a split-screen with Emerald, identified by bold white text.

Tonya (Ronnie's Girlfriend).description: A woman with long brown hair, wearing a light blue top, visibly crying with tears streaming down her face, her hand often covering her mouth or face in distress, appearing emotionally overwhelmed throughout the segment, speaking to express her frustration ('Didn't act like it'), screaming in distress, and repeatedly exclaiming 'I told you!' and questioning 'Is she too white now?' after the results are revealed.

Emerald.description: A young child with light brown hair, shown in close-up with a smiling expression, later appearing in a split-screen with Persais, identified by bold white text.

Framed Photo.description: A framed photo of a baby, displayed on a surface beside the Male Host during the initial confrontation, adding emotional weight to the paternity question.

Ronnie.description: A man with short dark hair and a mustache, wearing a red and black striped shirt, seated on a beige couch with a serious, stoic expression, shown in close-up and on a large screen behind the host, speaking briefly to express doubt ('They don't look nothing like me') and to agree with the host ('Yeah'), then reacting with shock and resignation, leaning forward and raising his arms in a dramatic gesture before embracing Tonya.

Audience Members.description: Diverse group of people in cutouts in the 'CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE' end-screen, displaying varied reactions—some covering their mouths in shock, others gasping, with expressions of disbelief and concern, whose collective, loud, and chaotic gasps, cheers, and shouts of 'Whoa!' and 'Oh my God!' are heard reacting to the dramatic announcement.

Young Man (Escardo).description: A young man with short dark hair and a goatee, shown in a cutout in the 'CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE' end-screen, wearing a black and red striped shirt, with a confident smirk.

Dark-Haired Woman.description: A woman with long dark hair, shown in a cutout in the 'CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE' end-screen, displaying a serious and concerned expression, possibly his mother or guardian.

Blonde Woman (Katherine).description: A young woman with shoulder-length blonde hair, shown in a cutout in the 'CLICK HERE TO SUBSCRIBE' end-screen, appearing emotional and distressed, with her mouth open as if reacting to the drama.

raw_segments: The segment opens on a talk show set with a brick wall backdrop and warm studio lighting, as the Male Host, in a black long-sleeve shirt, dramatically holds a yellow envelope labeled 'DDC DNA RESULTS'. On-screen text reads '3 TEEN MOMS...3 BABIES... WHICH TEEN BOY IS THE DAD?' as the Host declares, "When it comes to two-year-old Dayton, Dee, you are not the father!" This announcement is met with gasps, cheers, and applause from the audience. The camera focuses on Dee (Marshaun's Brother), a young Black man in a pink checkered shirt, who reacts with visible shock and distress, covering his face with his hands as the on-screen text confirms 'YOU ARE NOT THE FATHER!' and 'THE DNA TEST PROVED THAT DEE IS NOT THE FATHER OF BRANDY'S BABY'. Brandy, a young woman with blonde hair in a purple lace top, cries intensely, her face contorted in emotional pain, while Marshaun, a young Black man in a blue button-down shirt, reacts with disbelief, standing abruptly and walking away in distress. The Male Host gestures emphatically, attempting to manage the emotional chaos, then addresses Marshaun, speaking of "a lot of healing to do" and "forgiveness to be given," urging all three to see his counselor, Steve, to "give yourselves a chance as a family or not a family." The scene transitions to a new confrontation, with the Male Host now in a red sweater, introducing a new case: '2 TEEN COUSINS...2 BABIES... IS BONIFASIO THE FATHER?'. He welcomes a young woman with long dark hair, wearing a blue top, identifying her as "Tanya" to the show, noting that "Tanya admits, she made a mistake," as she walks beside him, covering her face in distress, all underscored by the charged red and orange studio lighting and the prominent 'Maury' logo.

The segment opens with a close-up of Tonya, Ronnie’s girlfriend, visibly distressed and crying on a beige couch, her hand covering her mouth, as on-screen text identifies her and states her purpose to prove Ronnie fathered both her children. The Male Host's voiceover begins, referencing events from 'six years ago' and Tonya 'paying for it ever since.' The scene cuts to Bonifasio, a man in a red and black striped shirt, sitting with a serious expression. The Male Host, wearing a red sweater, then appears, speaking with urgency and gesturing, introducing the case with on-screen text: '2 TEEN COUSINS...2 BABIES... IS BONIFASIO THE FATHER?' He continues his voiceover, explaining Tonya 'cheated one time' and Ronnie 'never did forgive her.' The camera returns to Tonya, tears streaming down her face, her expression one of anguish, as she begins to speak through sobs, stating, 'For seven years, I have loved and devoted my life to Ronnie.' A montage follows, showing split-screen images of two young children, Persais and Emerald, with their names displayed. Tonya's emotional voice continues, explaining her heart is 'broken because he doesn't think my four-year-old Emerald and my two-year-old Persais is his,' admitting she 'cheated on Ronnie' once, 'three years before our first daughter was born.' The children’s faces are shown in close-up, one pouting and the other smiling, before merging into a composite image with Tonya behind them, as the lighting shifts dramatically to red and dark tones. The Male Host reappears, speaking emphatically, while Tonya's tearful voice continues, lamenting that Ronnie 'pays more attention to Persais than he does to Emerald,' and how Emerald asks, 'Why doesn't Daddy want to spend time with me?' The segment ends with Tonya’s face in close-up, her eyes wide and tearful under the pulsing dramatic red lighting, as she cries, 'Right now, there's no family, and I can't take it anymore. I want my kids to be happy and have their dad in their life.' The Male Host then directly asks her, 'You all are together, right?' to which Tonya tearfully replies, 'Yes.' He follows up, 'So he's in the house all the time with those kids?' and Tonya clarifies, 'We don't live together.'

The segment opens with a close-up of Tonya, Ronnie's girlfriend, a woman with long brown hair in a light blue top, visibly distressed and crying as she admits to cheating on Ronnie over six years ago, stating in a tearful voice, "I was tired of it." The Male Host, a middle-aged man in a red sweater, probes her, asking if she moved out, to which she confirms, "No, I did." The studio audience reacts with gasps and cheers as the host emphasizes the core conflict: '2 TEEN COUSINS...2 BABIES... IS BONIFASIO THE FATHER?'. The camera cuts to Ronnie, a man with short dark hair and a mustache, wearing a red and black striped shirt, who listens with a stoic, serious expression.
